articles.title: Stocks Get Some Relief as Brent Slips Below $100: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: US stocks are finding some respite after posting their biggest drop this month as Brent crude eased below $100 a barrel and bond yields halted their ascent.

articles.title: Metals Fluctuate as Middle East Escalation Keeps Bulls in Check
articles.summary: Base metals fluctuated at the end of the week as the threat of an escalating conflict in the Middle East — and more turbulence for the global economy — kept a lid on optimism.

articles.title: Yen’s Worst Week Since May Brings It Close to 165 Versus Dollar
articles.summary: The yen is on track for its biggest weekly loss in more than two months, with intervention warnings doing little to deter investors betting on further weakness.

articles.title: Gold Edges Up as Traders Weigh Mideast Conflict, Fed Rate Path
articles.summary: Gold edged higher, holding firmly above the key resistance point of $4,000 an ounce even as the widening conflict in the Middle East fuels expectations for tighter monetary policy.

articles.title: Dangote Targets $100 Billion Empire With Oil and Fertilizers
articles.summary: Aliko Dangote is betting that Africa’s largest refinery, the continent’s biggest cement producer and his fertilizer businesses will expand his empire fivefold to $100 billion by the end of the decade.

articles.title: Wheat Extends July Rally as War Risks Stoke Fears Over Supply
articles.summary: Wheat climbed to a two-year high in Chicago and Paris, as escalating geopolitical tensions and worsening weather in key producing regions continued to stoke supply concerns.

articles.title: Travelers Draws Wave of Bears as Stock Eyes Best Year Since 2000
articles.summary: The best year for Travelers Companies Inc. shares in more than 25 years has unleashed a bevy of downgrades as Wall Street bets that the stock’s gains have peaked.
